raw_consumer_statement:
I have been attempting to resolve a recurring dispute with XXXX for several years now involving not XXXX, but XXXX of my American Express credit cards. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX charged my card ending in XXXX {$12.00} for services I do not have and have not used. I disputed the charge the same day, which was closed in my favor and I was refunded. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I was again charged {$12.00} to my card ending in XXXX. I did not realize it had been charged and therefore did not dispute it. 

Ten months later, on XX/XX/XXXX, I was charged {$12.00} a third time on my card ending in XXXX. I disputed the charge the same day. I was charged yet again on XX/XX/XXXX to my card ending in XXXX. I also disputed this charge. 

On XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, American Express closed my disputes in Yahoos favor and rebilled these amounts. XXXX apparently produced unsigned receipts for unidentified purchases, which American Express seem to have found were sufficient. XXXX did not produce a contract. 

I then attempted to contact XXXX through the phone number listed on the transaction. After being rerouted to no less than five different numbers, I finally contacted XXXX  corporate office and left a voicemail. I was then contacted through e-mail by XXXX of XXXX XXXX XXXX. Unsurprisingly, XXXX refused to communicate in writing and would only speak to me over the phone. During this phone conversation, XXXX informed me that I would no longer be charged, and processed refunds of both the XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX transactions and I considered the matter resolved. I also placed a merchant block on my card ending in XXXX. 

You can imagine my utter surprise and outrage when I was billed on a different card a total of {$60.00} over XXXX transactions on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX. My card ending in XXXX was charged {$12.00} on XX/XX/XXXX, {$24.00} on XX/XX/XXXX, {$12.00} on XX/XX/XXXX, and another {$12.00} transaction on XX/XX/XXXX. 

Upon further investigation into this issue, it appears that I overlooked similar charges from XXXX to my card ending in XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX ( {$12.00} ) and XX/XX/XXXX ( XXXX transactions totaling {$48.00} ). It is unclear how or why XXXX has indiscriminately decided to charge one card or the other. 

In total, I am owed {$120.00} to date by XXXX.
